text: The patient is positioned in right lateral decubitus for a laparoscopic partial splenectomy. Trocars are placed as in a classic total splenectomy. The surgeon approaches the splenic pedicle, dissecting the vessels individually after mobilizing the left colic angle.

The surgeon performs selective dissection of each vessel to minimize hemorrhage risk, revealing a visible line of demarcation between ischemic and vascularized sectors of the spleen. The dissection of the splenic pedicle continues until complete demarcation of the resected area is achieved.

The surgeon uses a harmonic scalpel to transect the splenic parenchyma in the de-vascularized zone. Hemostasis is achieved with a bipolar clamp, and a hemostatic compress is placed on the section. The excised part is extracted, a drain is left in place, and a control scan shows a well-vascularized spleen at four months post-operation.
